DEFENDANT HAS NOT SUSTAINED ITS BURDEN TO SHIFTED THE BURDEN TO PLAINTIFF TO DEFEAT THE MOTION. ANDREWS, 138 C.A.4TH 96, 106-107 DOES NOT APPLY, FOR HERE THERE IS NO ISSUE CONCERNING FAILURE TO IDENTIFY A MANUFACTURER. PLAINTIFF'S DISCOVERY RESPONSES ARE NOT 'FACTUALLY DEVOID' ON THE CENTRAL ISSUE POSED BY THIS MOTION, I.E., WHETHER DEFENDANT ACTED WITH DUE CARE OR REASONABLY. THE MATERIAL PROVIDED BY DEFENDANT (VICKERY'S ADDRESSES) DO NOT OBVIOUSLY RELATE TO ASBESTOS NOR TO ITS USE IN ENGINE ROOMS, NOR, MORE PARTICULARLY, TO THE LEVEL OF CARE DUE DURING THE TIME THE PLAINTIFF WAS ALLEGEDLY EXPOSED--- SOME THIRTY YEARS LATER AFTER THE SPEECHES. EVEN IF DEFENDANT HAD MET ITS INITIAL BURDEN ON THIS MOTION, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE OF HIS LIKELY CONTACT WITH ASBESTOS ON THE VESSEL IN QUESTION, AS WELL AS EVIDENCE OF KNOWLEDGE OF THE DANGERS OF ASBESTOS AT THE RELEVANT TIME (COHEN DEC. 7; BACKENSTOE DEC. EX J), AND DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO WARN. ORDER SIGNED AFTER SESSION CONCLUDES. JUDGE: CURTIS KARNOW, REPORTER: CARLITO MANDIA, CSR 	689

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-28-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SEARIVER MARITIME, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: WILLIAM F. RUIZ, ESQUIRE (BRAYTON PURCELL, 415.898.1555) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JENNIFER FITZPATRICK, ESQUIRE (ARMSTRONG & ASSOCIATES, 510.433.1830) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. Defendant has not sustained its burden to shifted the burden to plaintiff to defeat the motion. Andrews, 138 C.A.4th 96, 106-107 does not apply, for here there is no issue concerning failure to identify a manufacturer. Plaintiff's discovery responses are not 'factually devoid' on the central issue posed by this motion, i.e., whether defendant acted with due care or reasonably. The material provided by defendant (Vickery's addresses) do not obviously relate to asbestos nor to its use in engine rooms, nor, more particularly, to the level of care due during the time the plaintiff was allegedly exposed--- some thirty years later after the speeches. Even if defendant had met its initial burden on this motion, Plaintiff has presented sufficient evidence of his likely contact with asbestos on the vessel in question, as well as evidence of knowledge of the dangers of asbestos at the relevant time (Cohen Dec. 7; Backenstoe Dec. EX J), and defendant's failure to warn. ORDER SIGNED AFTER SESSION CONCLUDES. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
